message.content:
# Mobile App Screenshot Analysis: Mind & Psychology Section

## 1. Page Title and Navigation Structure
- **Top Bar**: Dark navy background with status icons (time: 1:07, battery: 91%, network: 5G/VoLTE)
- **Primary Navigation Tabs**: 
  - "Foundations" (inactive, dark gray)
  - "Mind & Psychology" (active, blue background)
- **Secondary Navigation Buttons**:
  - "Comparative Minds" (active, blue background)
  - "Wellness Tools" (inactive, dark gray)
- **Section Header**: "THE THREE MINDS" with brain icon
- **Subsection Tabs**:
  - "Time Perception" (active, blue background)
  - "Stress Response" (inactive, dark gray)
  - "Thought Patterns" (inactive, dark gray, partially visible)

## 2. Visible Text Content
- "Foundations"
- "Mind & Psychology"
- "Comparative Minds"
- "Wellness Tools"
- "THE THREE MINDS"
- "How different mindsets experience the same reality"
- "Time Perception"
- "Stress Response"
- "Thought Patterns"
- "Balanced"
- "Past is processed, present is engaged, future is hopeful. Flexible attention that moves naturally between time horizons. Plans feel achievable and motivating."
- "Trapped"
- "Rigidly locked in short-term survival mode. The past is a source of shame, the present is unbearable, and the future feels impossible. Time feels both endless and fleeting — an agonizing paradox of hypofrontality."
- "Rebuilding"
- "Learning to be present again. The past is being reframed through self-compassion rather than shame. Future thinking returns gradually as the prefrontal cortex heals — one day, one hour at a time."
- Bottom navigation: "Home", "Substances", "Bio Tools", "Recovery", "PH Guide"
- Browser bar: "recovery-line.vercel.app"

## 3. Categories and Menu Items
- Main Categories: Foundations, Mind & Psychology
- Subcategories: Comparative Minds, Wellness Tools
- Section Topics: Time Perception, Stress Response, Thought Patterns
- Mind States: Balanced, Trapped, Rebuilding
- Bottom Navigation: Home, Substances, Bio Tools, Recovery, PH Guide

## 4. Data Visualizations
- Three colored cards representing different mind states:
  - Green card: "Balanced" (positive state)
  - Red card: "Trapped" (negative state)
  - Blue card: "Rebuilding" (recovery state)
- Each card contains descriptive text explaining the mental state

## 5. Interactive Elements
- Top navigation tabs (Foundations, Mind & Psychology)
- Secondary navigation buttons (Comparative Minds, Wellness Tools)
- Subsection tabs (Time Perception, Stress Response, Thought Patterns)
- Bottom navigation icons (Home, Substances, Bio Tools, Recovery, PH Guide)
- Floating action button (red circular button with phone icon in bottom right)
- Browser navigation (back, forward, refresh, menu buttons)
- Browser address bar with lock icon

## 6. Color Scheme and Visual Design
- **Primary Background**: Dark navy (#1a2332 or similar)
- **Active Elements**: Blue (#2563eb or similar)
- **Inactive Elements**: Dark gray (#374151 or similar)
- **Cards**:
  - Balanced: Dark green background (#065f46)
  - Trapped: Dark red background (#7f1d1d)
  - Rebuilding: Dark blue background (#1e3a8a)
- **Text Colors**:
  - White/light gray for main text
  - Colored text for section titles (green, red, blue)
- **Icons**: White or light gray on dark backgrounds
- **Borders**: Subtle rounded corners on cards
- **Status Bar**: Light gray background

## 7. Data Organization
- **Hierarchical Structure**:
  - Top-level navigation tabs
  - Secondary navigation buttons
  - Section header with icon
  - Subsection tabs
  - Card-based content (3 cards in vertical stack)
- **Layout**: Vertical scrolling content with fixed bottom navigation
- **Card Design**: Each card has colored background, icon, title, and descriptive text

## 8. Screen Layout Structure
- **Header**: Status bar + navigation tabs
- **Body**: 
  - Secondary navigation
  - Section title with icon
  - Subsection tabs
  - Content cards (3)
- **Footer**: Bottom navigation bar + browser interface

## 9. Numbers and Metrics
- Time: 1:07
- Battery: 91%
- Network: 5G/VoLTE

## 10. Icons and Meanings
- Brain icon: Represents "THE THREE MINDS" section
- Clock icon: Time Perception tab
- Brain/Neuron icon: Stress Response tab
- Chat bubble icon: Thought Patterns tab
- Home icon: Navigation to home screen
- Pill icon: Substances section
- Shield icon: Bio Tools section
- Heart icon: Recovery section (active)
- Map pin icon: PH Guide section
- Phone icon: Floating action button (likely contact/support)
- Browser navigation icons: Standard web browser controls

## Additional Details
- The interface appears to be a mental health or psychology app focused on recovery
- The "Recovery" tab is highlighted in the bottom navigation, indicating the current section
- The floating red button with phone icon suggests immediate contact or support functionality
- The browser bar indicates this might be a web-based app or PWA (Progressive Web App)
- The design uses a dark theme with high contrast for readability
- Cards have subtle rounded corners and padding for visual separation
- Text is left-aligned within cards
- Iconography is minimal and functional, using standard symbols for clarity
